author: ayumi.meade
body: Please ensure the acknowledgment section references the updated code of conduct language approved by the Board in June. Angela, can you confirm the correct Board action number?

author: angela.marsh
body: The Board action is Resolution 2025-42, adopted June 24, 2025. I'll add the reference to the acknowledgment section.

author: grace.whitmore
body: We should add a note clarifying that OFLA runs concurrently with FMLA where both apply. Laura, please verify with legal before finalizing.

author: laura.kim
body: Confirmed with district counsel. OFLA and FMLA run concurrently for qualifying reasons. I'll add a sidebar with the comparison chart.

author: laura.kim
body: The Danielson Framework alignment table needs to be updated to reflect the revised Domain 3 indicators from the 2024 edition. I'll have a draft to Grace by the end of the week.

author: grace.whitmore
body: Thank you Laura. Also confirm that the February 15 deadline is still accurate given the calendar shift — some principals flagged a conflict with testing week.

author: angela.marsh
body: Should we include the quorum requirements explicitly? State law changed in 2025 — the new minimum for a 7-member board is 4, not a simple majority of those present.

author: ayumi.meade
body: Good catch Angela. Please add a quorum section referencing ORS 332.045 and note that Patricia Odom reviewed this language with district counsel.

author: angela.marsh
body: Reminder that the 72-hour agenda posting requirement applies to the full packet including attachments. Last cycle two attachments were missing from the initial post.

author: ayumi.meade
body: Agreed. I've asked Angela to build a pre-meeting checklist to ensure all attachments are uploaded before the 72-hour window opens. Will link it from this page once complete.

author: grace.whitmore
body: The fee schedule referenced here was last updated in 2023. With inflation adjustments pending, should we note that fees are under review for 2025-2026?

author: ayumi.meade
body: Yes, please add a note that the fee schedule is under Board review and link to the draft once Marcus has the updated schedule ready.

author: grace.whitmore
body: The drill log submission deadline should be 24 hours, not 48 hours, based on the updated Oregon rule effective January 2025. Please correct.

author: dev.patel
body: Also worth noting that the reunification site for Madison Elementary changed in March 2025 to Cascade Ridge Community Center. Each school's EOP should be checked for accuracy.

author: diane.foster
body: Updating the response timeline section to reflect the new same-day standard for social media inquiries in addition to breaking news. Digital news cycles require a faster turnaround.

author: ayumi.meade
body: The 30-minute activation window is ambitious. Recommend we specify it as a target and include an escalation path if the Communications Director is unreachable.

author: diane.foster
body: Agreed. Adding a backup contact chain: Communications Director, then Deputy Superintendent, then Superintendent. Will update the protocol accordingly.

author: marcus.bell
body: Note that Oregon Budget Law requires the Budget Committee to include the same number of appointed citizen members as Board members. With seven Board seats, we need seven citizen members this cycle.

author: marcus.bell
body: The $25,000 Board approval threshold aligns with ORS 279B.085. Legal reviewed this section on October 10, 2025. No changes needed to the threshold but we should note the statute citation explicitly.

author: ayumi.meade
body: Marcus, please also clarify how this interacts with multi-year contracts — a contract that totals $100,000 over three years but is $30,000 annually should still trigger the full threshold analysis.

author: grace.whitmore
body: The Whole Child pillar timeline needs a note that the mental health services expansion is contingent on the state grant award expected in March 2026. Should we flag this dependency?

author: ayumi.meade
body: Yes, add a funding note under Pillar 2 indicating the phased rollout is dependent on the Oregon Student Success Act grant renewal. I'll confirm status with Marcus and update the timeline by end of September.
